title: A Quiet Place: Day One Box Office Performance
text: A Quiet Place: Day One achieved a strong debut with $53 million in its first three days of release, surpassing both previous franchise entries. The original A Quiet Place opened to $50 million, while A Quiet Place Part II earned $47 million domestically. The horror prequel received favorable reviews, earning an 84% fresh approval rating on Rotten Tomatoes and a B+ CinemaScore from opening audiences. Collider's review highlighted the film's brilliant characterization, simple but effective script, and outstanding performances. Directed by Michael Sarnoski and starring Lupita Nyong'o and Joseph Quinn, the prequel was produced on a reported budget of around $60 million. Despite strong competition, Inside Out 2 retained the number one spot with an estimated $56 million in its third weekend, while Kevin Costner's Horizon: An American Saga Chapter 1 opened in third place with $11 million. Together, the top two films generated over $100 million that weekend.
